items.hook: ‘The minute I had success, I stopped taking drugs’: John Waters on 60 years of screen carnage
items.body:
As Hairspray and his ‘angriest movie’ Desperate Living are rereleased, the ‘Pope of Trash’ reflects on dead dogs, dirty rats, ‘that lunatic RFK’ and why there are no novelty dances any more

items.hook: Guilty Men is a British polemical book written under the pseudonym "Cato" that was published in July 1940, after the failure of British forces to prevent the defeat and occupation of Norway and France by Nazi Germany.
items.body: It attacked fifteen public figures for their failed policies towards Germany and for their failure to re-equip the British armed forces. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: American Civil War: At the order of President Abraham Lincoln, Union troops begin a 25-mile march into Virginia for what will become the First Battle of Bull Run, the first major land battle of the war.
items.body: The American Civil War was a civil war in the United States between the Union and the Confederacy, which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: At Treasure Island on the Delaware River in the United States, the First Order of the Arrow ceremony takes place and the Order of the Arrow is founded to honor American Boy Scouts who best exemplify the Scout Oath and Law.
items.body: The Delaware River is a major river in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States and is the longest free-flowing (undammed) river in the Eastern United States. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Kep1er is a South Korean girl group formed by CJ ENM through the Mnet reality competition show Girls Planet 999 in 2021.
items.body: The group consists of six members: Kim Chae-hyun, Huening Bahiyyih, Choi Yu-jin, Kim Da-yeon, Ezaki Hikaru, and Shen Xiaoting. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Diocese of North America & Europe is a diocese of the Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Church that includes all the parishes in the North American and European continents.
items.body: Its headquarters is at Sinai Mar Thoma Centre, Merrick, New York. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Manchu Qing dynasty naval forces under commander Shi Lang defeat the Kingdom of Tungning in the Battle of Penghu near the Pescadores Islands.
items.body: The Qing dynasty, officially the Great Qing, also known as the Qing Empire or Qing China, was a Manchu-led imperial dynasty of China and an early modern empire in East Asia which existed from 1636/1644 to 1912. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: ‘People are picking the dumbest fights’: the tortured history of America’s culture wars
items.body:
In a new book, Isaac Butler goes back to the 1980s to trace how battles started against the arts, from Piss Christ to Mapplethorpe, and looks at what we can learn for today

items.hook: The Parliament of the Ukrainian SSR declares state sovereignty over the territory of the Ukrainian SSR.
items.body: The Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine is the unicameral parliament of Ukraine. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The last apparition of the Blessed Virgin Mary to Bernadette Soubirous in Lourdes, France.
items.body: The Lourdes apparitions are several Marian apparitions reported in 1858 by Bernadette Soubirous, the 14-year-old daughter of a miller, in the town of Lourdes in Southern France. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: There he studied the natural history of the Atlantic forest and was an early advocate of Darwinism.
items.body: Johann Friedrich Theodor Müller, better known as Fritz Müller, and also as Müller-Desterro, was a German biologist who emigrated to southern Brazil, where he lived in and near the city of Blumenau, Santa Catarina. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Jacques Cartier, navigator and explorer, returns home to St. Malo after claiming Stadacona (Quebec), Hochelaga (Montreal) and the River of Canada (St. Lawrence River) region for France.
items.body: Jacques Cartier was a French maritime explorer from Brittany. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Hans Peter Schmid is a climatologist.
items.body: He is director of the Institute of Meteorology and Climate Research - Atmospheric Environmental Research of the Karlsruhe Institute of Technology (KIT/IMK-IFU) based in Garmisch-Partenkirchen. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: In the mathematical field of graph theory, the Brinkmann graph is a 4-regular graph with 21 vertices and 42 edges discovered by Gunnar Brinkmann in 1992.
items.body: It was first published by Brinkmann and Meringer in 1997. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Sam Neill’s cause of death revealed to be pneumonia
items.body: Neill’s longtime representative says the actor’s family agreed to release the information due to ‘inaccuracies and outright falsehoods’ in media
